refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the measurement/verification aspect from the capability delivery aspect, and explicitly acknowledges dual operator modes (on-site and tele-operated) as distinct execution contexts. This clarification is essential because productivity depends on both machine capability and operator effectiveness, which differ between direct and remote control.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials under agreed conditions (soil type, task profile, weather, operator experience level). Measure actual throughput (m³/h or ton/h) and compare against productivity_target. Log operator mode (on-site vs. remote), task duration, material moved, and any safety/policy violations. Establish baseline and delta metrics.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall provide sufficient power, speed, and digging/loading capacity to move material at rates consistent with productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The on-site Operator Interface shall enable intuitive, responsive control of the Vehicle to maximize task execution efficiency.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Teleoperation system shall enable remote operators to control the Vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient to maintain productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The Safety Services subsystem shall enforce worksite safety plan and operational policies without degrading productivity below acceptable thresholds.
FR-NGHE-001.5: The system shall provide real-time Telemetry to Site Manager and Tele-operator for task monitoring and productivity tracking.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-power hydraulic earthmoving vehicle with responsive operator controls (on-site) and a low-latency teleoperation gateway (remote) to deliver material movement rates meeting productivity_target; enforce safety constraints via real-time monitoring and task interlocks.

DP-B: Deploy a modular vehicle platform with configurable bucket/blade capacity and engine power, selectable by task type; couple with dual-mode operator interface (direct + remote) and adaptive safety policies that scale constraints based on operator skill level and task risk.

DP-C: Implement a productivity-optimized control architecture that prioritizes throughput within safety envelope: high-responsiveness hydraulic actuation, predictive load sensing, operator skill-adaptive assistance, and remote teleoperation with sub-100ms latency; safety constraints are enforced as hard limits on machine state, not task blocking.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Implement a productivity-optimized control architecture that prioritizes throughput within safety envelope: high-responsiveness hydraulic actuation, predictive load sensing, operator skill-adaptive assistance, and remote teleoperation with sub-100ms latency; safety constraints are enforced as hard limits on machine state, not task blocking.

potential_sources: Coupling between operator skill adaptation and safety constraint enforcement: adaptive assistance must not mask unsafe operator behavior. Mitigation: safety limits are hard machine-state constraints, independent of skill model.

Coupling between teleoperation latency and productivity: sub-100ms latency is achievable with modern network infrastructure but depends on Network Link quality. Mitigation: graceful degradation and operator notification if latency exceeds threshold.
Coupling between hydraulic responsiveness and safety: high-responsiveness actuation increases risk of unintended motion. Mitigation: dual-channel control validation and fail-safe valve design.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C is selected because it explicitly decouples productivity optimization (control responsiveness, skill adaptation) from safety enforcement (hard machine-state limits). This avoids the false trade-off of sacrificing safety for throughput. The architecture is implementable with current technology and aligns with high safety criticality constraint.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Operator skill levels can be reliably classified and adaptive assistance tuned without introducing new failure modes.
ASSUMPTION: Sub-100ms teleoperation latency is achievable over the Network Link under representative worksite conditions.
ASSUMPTION: Hydraulic actuation can be designed with sufficient redundancy and fail-safe behavior to meet high safety criticality.
RISK: If skill classification is inaccurate, adaptive assistance may over-assist novice operators or under-assist experts, degrading productivity or increasing safety risk.
RISK: Network congestion or interference could violate latency requirement, forcing fallback to on-site operation only.
RISK: Hard machine-state safety limits may be perceived as overly restrictive by operators, leading to workarounds or non-compliance.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to explicitly define the safety constraint enforcement mechanism (e.g., state machine, interlocks, rate limiters) and the skill classification algorithm. Specify fallback behavior if teleoperation latency exceeds threshold. Conduct human factors analysis to validate that hard safety limits are acceptable to operators.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The complete integrated system encompassing Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and their interconnections. This is the top-level implementation module that realizes the productivity KPI requirement through coordinated subsystem behavior.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time machine state, productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h), operator mode, safety status, and task progress transmitted to Site Manager and Tele-operator for monitoring and decision-making.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Task definitions, productivity targets, worksite safety plan constraints, and operational policies received from Site Manager or planning system.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Real-time safety alerts, constraint violations, and emergency notifications transmitted to Safety Manager and on-site personnel.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication channel for teleoperation commands, telemetry, and safety signals between on-site Vehicle and remote Tele-operator.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integration module that contains all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and their coordination logic. Productivity KPI is a system-level property that emerges from integrated behavior. Containment is very high because the requirement is explicitly about the complete solution. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary.

Vehicle provides the physical capability (power, speed, digging capacity) necessary for productivity, but does not independently achieve the KPI. Productivity also depends on operator control quality (Teleoperation, on-site interface) and safety constraint enforcement (Safety Services). Assigning to Vehicle alone would diffuse the requirement across multiple subsystems without clear ownership of the integrated productivity target.

Teleoperation enables remote operator control and contributes to productivity by extending operator reach, but does not own the overall productivity KPI. Productivity depends equally on Vehicle capability, on-site operator interface, and safety constraints. Assigning to Teleoperation alone would create false ownership and obscure the system-level integration requirement.

Safety Services enforces constraints that affect productivity (e.g., rate limiters, interlocks) but does not own the productivity target itself. Safety is a constraint on productivity, not the driver. Assigning to Safety Services would invert the requirement hierarchy and create confusion about primary vs. secondary objectives.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The productivity KPI (CR-NGHE-001) is a system-level acceptance criterion that depends on integrated behavior across Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and operator interaction. No single subsystem can independently achieve or verify the KPI. Therefore, ownership must reside at the NGHE Solution level, with clear allocation of sub-requirements to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. This ensures that productivity is treated as an emergent system property, not a component-level specification.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution is treated as a passive container rather than an active integration module, responsibility for productivity coordination may be unclear. Mitigation: define explicit integration control logic (e.g., task scheduler, mode manager, constraint enforcer) within NGHE Solution.

RISK: Productivity measurement and verification may be difficult if interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking) are not well-defined. Mitigation: specify Telemetry interface to include real-time throughput metrics, task status, and operator mode.

RISK: Safety constraints enforced by Safety Services may conflict with productivity optimization. Mitigation: design safety constraints as hard machine-state limits (not task-level blocking) to minimize productivity impact while maintaining safety.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Explicitly define the NGHE Solution integration module as containing: (1) Task Scheduler: receives Tasking input, allocates tasks to Vehicle, monitors progress via Telemetry; (2) Mode Manager: switches between on-site and remote operator modes based on Network Link availability and operator input; (3) Constraint Enforcer: applies safety limits from Safety Services to Vehicle actuation commands; (4) Telemetry Aggregator: collects machine state, productivity metrics, and safety status, transmits via Telemetry interface. This clarifies the integration logic and makes productivity ownership explicit.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (productivity-optimized control architecture with hard safety limits) directly addresses the refined FR by enabling high-responsiveness operator control (on-site and remote) while enforcing safety constraints. The DP explicitly decouples productivity optimization from safety enforcement, avoiding false trade-offs. Assumptions about skill classification, latency, and hydraulic design are documented and manageable.

diffusion_risks: DP-C specifies control architecture (responsiveness, skill adaptation, latency) but does not explicitly map to module boundaries. Risk: implementation may diffuse the control logic across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services without clear ownership. Mitigation: assign control architecture to NGHE Solution integration module (Task Scheduler, Mode Manager, Constraint Enforcer) and define clear interfaces to Vehicle and Safety Services.

Teleoperation latency requirement (sub-100ms) depends on Network Link quality, which is external to NGHE Solution. Risk: if Network Link is degraded, latency requirement cannot be met, forcing fallback to on-site operation. Mitigation: define graceful degradation behavior and operator notification in Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary because it contains the integration logic (Task Scheduler, Mode Manager, Constraint Enforcer) that realizes DP-C. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-modules that implement specific aspects of the DP. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and do not diffuse the requirement.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Productivity KPI can be verified through representative operations trials: measure actual throughput (m³/h or ton/h) under agreed conditions and compare against productivity_target. Telemetry interface provides real-time metrics. Verification is feasible if task definition, soil conditions, operator experience, and evaluation period are clearly specified in Tasking input.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Refined FR is decomposed into five sub-FRs (Vehicle capability, on-site operator interface, teleoperation fidelity, safety constraint enforcement, telemetry provision). Each sub-FR is allocated to a specific subsystem (Vehicle, on-site interface, Teleoperation, Safety Services, NGHE Solution integration). Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-C to explicitly define the control architecture components (Task Scheduler, Mode Manager, Constraint Enforcer) and their interfaces to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.
Specify the skill classification algorithm and adaptive assistance tuning parameters. Conduct human factors analysis to validate operator acceptance.

Define the teleoperation latency requirement (sub-100ms) and fallback behavior if Network Link degrades. Specify Network Link quality metrics (bandwidth, jitter, packet loss) required to meet latency.
Specify the hard machine-state safety limits (e.g., maximum speed, maximum digging force, rate limiters) and their enforcement mechanism (e.g., state machine, interlocks).
Define Telemetry interface to include real-time throughput metrics (m³/h or ton/h), task status, operator mode, safety status, and latency measurements.

decision.justification: The refined FR is decomposed into five atomic sub-FRs that address distinct aspects of the productivity KPI: Vehicle capability, operator control (on-site and remote), safety constraint enforcement, and telemetry provision. Each sub-FR is ready for the next level of functional decomposition. DP-C is sound and well-justified, but requires refinement to explicitly map control architecture components to module boundaries. NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary, but requires explicit definition of integration logic (Task Scheduler, Mode Manager, Constraint Enforcer). Proceeding to the next decomposition level will clarify the control architecture and allocate sub-FRs to subsystems.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall provide sufficient power, speed, and digging/loading capacity to move material at rates consistent with productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The on-site Operator Interface shall enable intuitive, responsive control of the Vehicle to maximize task execution efficiency.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Teleoperation system shall enable remote operators to control the Vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient to maintain productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The Safety Services subsystem shall enforce worksite safety plan and operational policies without degrading productivity below acceptable thresholds.
FR-NGHE-001.5: The system shall provide real-time Telemetry to Site Manager and Tele-operator for task monitoring and productivity tracking.

revise.why: DP-C specifies the control architecture concept but does not explicitly define the integration components (Task Scheduler, Mode Manager, Constraint Enforcer) and their interfaces. Revision should map DP-C to the NGHE Solution integration module and define clear ownership of control logic. This will reduce ambiguity in the next decomposition level.

assumptions: Operator skill levels can be reliably classified and adaptive assistance tuned without introducing new failure modes.
Sub-100ms teleoperation latency is achievable over the Network Link under representative worksite conditions.
Hydraulic actuation can be designed with sufficient redundancy and fail-safe behavior to meet high safety criticality.
Hard machine-state safety limits are acceptable to operators and do not create unintended workarounds.
Productivity measurement (m³/h or ton/h) can be accurately captured via Telemetry interface during representative operations.
Worksite safety plan and operational policies can be translated into machine-level constraints (e.g., speed limits, digging force limits, geofences).
open_risks: Skill classification algorithm may be inaccurate, leading to over-assistance or under-assistance.
Network Link degradation could violate teleoperation latency requirement, forcing fallback to on-site operation.
Hard safety limits may be perceived as overly restrictive, leading to operator non-compliance or workarounds.
Productivity target may be unachievable due to soil conditions, task complexity, or operator experience level.
Safety constraint enforcement may introduce latency or jitter in operator control, degrading productivity.
Telemetry interface may not capture all factors affecting productivity (e.g., operator fatigue, equipment wear, environmental conditions).
evidence_needed: Specification of skill classification algorithm and adaptive assistance tuning parameters.
Network Link quality metrics (bandwidth, jitter, packet loss) and latency measurements under representative worksite conditions.
Human factors analysis validating operator acceptance of hard safety limits.
Specification of machine-level safety constraints (speed limits, digging force limits, rate limiters, geofences) derived from worksite safety plan.
Representative operations trial data showing actual throughput (m³/h or ton/h) under agreed conditions.
Telemetry interface specification including real-time metrics, sampling rates, and data formats.
Hydraulic system design documentation including redundancy, fail-safe behavior, and response time specifications.
